children.heading: Report by Secretary
children.label: a Report by Secretary
children.text: Not later than , and biennially thereafter, the Secretary shall prepare and submit to the Committee on Education and the Workforce of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions of the Senate a report that contains a summary and analysis of the data and information provided to the Secretary in the State reports submitted under . Such report shall include an assessment, and where appropriate, recommendations for the Congress concerning efforts that should be undertaken to improve the access of the public to quality and affordable child care in the United States. Such report shall contain a determination around whether each State that uses amounts provided under this subchapter has complied with the priority for services described in sections 9858c(c)(2)(Q) and 9858c(c)(3)(B) of this title.July 31, 2016section 9858i of this title

children.heading: National toll-free hotline and Web site
children.label: b National toll-free hotline and Web site
A
children.text: develop and disseminate publicly available child care consumer education information for parents and help parents access safe and quality child care services in their community, with a range of price options, that best suits their family’s needs; and1So in original. Probably should be “suit”.1

children.label: B
children.text: to allow persons to report (anonymously if desired) suspected child abuse or neglect, or violations of health and safety requirements, by an eligible child care provider that receives assistance under this subchapter or a member of the provider’s staff.

children.heading: Referral to local child care providers
children.label: A Referral to local child care providers
children.text: The Web site shall be hosted by “childcare.gov”. The Web site shall enable a child care consumer to enter a zip code and obtain a referral to local child care providers described in subparagraph (B) within a specified search radius.

children.label: i
children.text: a localized list of all eligible child care providers, differentiating between licensed and license-exempt providers;

children.label: ii
children.text: any provider-specific information from a Quality Rating and Improvement System or information about other quality indicators, to the extent the information is publicly available and to the extent practicable;

children.label: iii
children.text: any other provider-specific information about compliance with licensing, and health and safety requirements to the extent the information is publicly available and to the extent practicable;

children.label: iv
children.text: referrals to local resource and referral organizations from which consumers can find more information about child care providers; and

children.label: v
children.text: State information about child care subsidy programs and other financial supports available to families.

children.heading: Nationwide capacity
children.label: C Nationwide capacity
children.text: The Web site and hotline shall have the capacity to help families in every State and community in the Nation.

children.heading: Information at all hours
children.label: D Information at all hours
children.text: The Web site shall provide, to parents and families, access to information about child care services 24 hours a day.

children.heading: Services in different languages
children.label: E Services in different languages
children.text: The Web site and hotline shall ensure the widest possible access to services for families who speak languages other than English.

children.heading: High-quality consumer education and referral
children.label: F High-quality consumer education and referral
children.text: The Web site and hotline shall ensure that families have access to easy-to-understand child care consumer education and referral services.

children.heading: Prohibition
children.label: 3 Prohibition
children.text: Nothing in this subsection shall be construed to allow the Secretary to compel States to provide additional data and information that is currently (as of ) not publicly available, or is not required by this subchapter, unless such additional data are related to the purposes and scope of this subchapter, and are subject to a notice and comment period of no less than 90 days.November 19, 2014
